creator: mmuir@es.co.nz
text:
Will Andrews wrote:
> 
> 
> This is cool!  I use wmhm on one of my machines.
> 
> > +# Version required:    1.44
> 
> Don't do this.  We changed our policy on versioning - this string is now
> stored in PORTVERSION variable.

Okay.
 
> I will handle this tomorrow..

Before you do.. 1.45 is already ready <dumb grin>..
I was going to wait until 1.44 was committed and then provide a diff to
that, but considering 1.43 was never committed due to slowness, should i
just let you superscede ports/18642 with a 1.42->1.45 ?
Heres the new stuff btw:

Version 1.45
        * Whoops, forgot to set the display on the cpu temp panel to
	  'CPUS'.. this was because i didnt have the gimp installed.. but
	  i do now!
        * Now you only click on the flashing mb temp bar to disable and
 	  enable the alarm. The reason for this is so that you can still
	  control the appearance and select different voltages etc while the alarm is 
	  going.
        * To show when the alarm is being ignored, the degree symbol will
	  turn red.
        * Alarm stops sounding if temp drops below threshold now.
        * Fixed flashing temp bar graph for alternate colourmaps.

	-mike
